chunk_summary: This section discusses life-course prevention opportunities in routine pediatric care in India, focusing on key risk windows (infancy, school transition, and adolescence) to prevent overnutrition and obesity through anticipatory guidance and practical interventions.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of anticipatory guidance during key life-course windows to prevent childhood obesity.
Identify risk factors for overnutrition in infancy, school-age children, and adolescents.
Learn practical strategies for pediatricians to promote healthy growth and prevent obesity in routine care.
Recognize the role of sleep, physical activity, and dietary habits in obesity prevention.
key_facts: Rapid weight gain in infancy can result from bottle-feeding, sugary drinks, and screen-assisted feeding.
Routine growth monitoring can identify upward crossing of weight-for-length or BMI trajectories early.
School transition increases access to ultraprocessed snacks and reduces free play, requiring preventive strategies like water-only beverages and no screens during meals.
Adolescence is a critical window due to circadian delay, academic pressure, and late-night phone use disrupting sleep and increasing snacking.
WHO 24-hour movement guidelines emphasize active play, sleep routines, and limiting sedentary behavior for children under 5 years.
reference_quote: The most effective preventive intervention is anticipatory guidance at predictable life-course windows.
document_key_topics: Obesity risk factors in infancy, school-age, and adolescence
Practical strategies for obesity prevention in India
